insight: For South Korea, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have not provided the desired boost, as they failed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean change of -0.200 minutes per minute, indicating a slight decline in performance. When South Korea was pressing before a break, they experienced an even sharper decline, with an average change of -0.300 minutes per minute, and were unable to regain their momentum after the break. In contrast, when they were not pressing before a break, the decline was less severe, with an average change of -0.100 minutes per minute, suggesting that the team may be better off conserving energy before a hydration break. Overall, these statistics suggest that hydration breaks have been a challenging period for South Korea, and the team may need to adjust their strategy to capitalize on these pauses in play.
